sections.title: Page
sections.text: Location Original text  Printed text  3 Section 7.3 Labelling of non-retail containers Information for non-retail containers shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers in Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to all fermented cooked cassava -based products, which are intended for direct human consumption and obtained after processing of cassava roots ( Manihot esculenta Crantz). This standard does not apply to gari.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Fermented cooked cassava-based products are presented in the form of cassava ball or sticks. These products are obtained from fresh cassava roots, peeled, cut, soaked in water for fermentation and pressed and dried before packaging and cooking.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the  General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)1 and the Code of Practice for the Reduction of Hydrocyanic Acid (HCN) in Cassava and Cassava Products (CXC 73-2013).2, 2 Fermented cooked cassava -based products shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the applicable sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene  (CXC 1-1969)3 and other relevant texts such as Codex Alimentarius codes of hygienic practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).4

sections.title: General quality factors
sections.text: Fermented cooked cassava -based products shall be clean and safe for human consumption. The moisture content should be characteristic of a given product. They shall be free from abnormal smell and taste, free of any foreign material, such as insect fragments, rodent hairs, grains of sand and dirt dust.

pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento, cabeçalho com código, e três seções principais sem numeração clara. Identificado título principal e seções '2022 Amendments' e 'Page' com texto associado. Seção '2022 Amendments' continua na próxima página.

Página com hierarquia clara de seções numeradas. Identificados níveis 1 e 2. Textos associados corretamente às seções e subseções. Cabeçalho com código do documento e paginação. Sem indicações de continuidade entre páginas.

Página apresenta título principal METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING com texto associado 'To be developed.' e seção NOTES com lista de referências. Cabeçalho indica código do documento e página. Não há continuidade de conteúdo entre páginas para estas seções.
